title: ParsedModel
description: Top-level parsed YAML model. Mirrors the on-disk YAML 1:1.

canonical_inputs.description: Phase 3C: optional always-load input set (sibling CSV or inline rows). Replaces the `mc-cli/main.rs` Acme-name special case. Models without this block load identically to Phase 3B.

lookup_tables.description: Lookup tables keyed by dimension element.

parameters.description: Phase 3J item 3: optional named scalar constants referenced by `param(name)` in formulas. v1 supports only `f64` values per ADR-0016 Decision 6 + Amendment §1; computed parameters and scoped parameters remain deferred to Phase 3J.1 (Amendment §2).

rules.description: Phase 3J item 4: rules default to empty Vec to allow models that declare only `Indicator` measures (which carry synthesized rule bodies). Pre-3J models always declared `rules:` explicitly; adding the default is purely additive.

status_thresholds.description: Status threshold bands for bucket() evaluation.

test_fixtures.description: Phase 3C: optional named per-test input fixtures. Each fixture is referenced by `golden_tests[i].fixture` for override semantics.

ParsedActualRefBody.description:
`actual_ref(Measure_Name)` — cross-coordinate read (Scenario shift).

Phase 3J item 6 extends this to optionally hold a `fallback` expression: `actual_ref(measure, fallback_expr)`. If the actual_ref read returns Null, `fallback` is evaluated lazily. Per ADR-0016 Amendment §3, the fallback may itself contain cross-coord functions (e.g., `scenario_ref`) — MC1013's nesting prohibition is explicitly relaxed for this slot only.

fallback.description: Phase 3J item 6: optional lazy fallback expression. Absent (None) preserves the Phase 3E 1-arg form's behavior. Boxed so the body type stays sized.

ParsedAdstockSpec.description: Phase 3H.2: geometric adstock spec for one feature. Per ADR-0018 Decision 2, both `rate` and `max_lookback` are required (no sensible defaults). Validators MC2075/MC2076 cover bounds; MC2071 covers `feature` membership in `coefficients`.

ParsedBinopBody.description: Shared body for binary operators (comparisons + logical and/or). The two children are boxed to allow recursive nesting.

ParsedCalibrationPoint.description: One point in a PAVA calibration map.

ParsedCurrentElementBody.description: Phase 3J item 2: `current_element(Dim)` — returns the current coord's element name in `Dim` as `Str`. Structured form: `{ current_element: "Channel" }`.

actuals_element.description: Phase 3E: for `kind: "Scenario"` dims, declares which element `actual_ref()` reads from. E.g., `actuals_element: "Actual"`.

description.description: Optional human-readable description. Phase 3B's MC3001 lint fires when this is missing on a Standard / Measure / Scenario / Version dim. The field is additive over the ADR-0004 schema; Phase 3A models with no descriptions still parse cleanly.

element_type.description: Phase 4C: optional element type constraint. `"string"` (default, no validation), `"date"` (elements must parse as time periods), `"numeric"` (elements must parse as numbers). Per ADR-0026 + TM1 comparison Part 7.

granularity.description: Phase 3F.1: declared granularity for Time dims. Legal values: `"day"` | `"week"` | `"month"` | `"quarter"` | `"year"`.

time_anchor.description: Phase 3F.1: runtime "now" anchor element name. Anchor functions (`anchor_index`, `is_past`, etc.) reference this element.

ParsedElement.description:
One element within a dimension. The optional fields are populated only when the parent dim's kind matches:

- `version_state` → Version dim (`"Draft" | "Submitted" | "Approved" | "Archived"`) - `scenario_meta` → Scenario dim (`"Default" | "NonDefault"`)

(Measure-dim elements are NOT modeled here — measures live under the top-level `measures:` block per the schema; a Measure dim with no elements declared inline is filled in from `measures:` by the validator.)

date.description: Phase 3F: optional ISO date metadata for Time-kind dimensions. Used by lint MC3010 (non-chronological order warning).

period_end_exclusive.description: Phase 3F.1: ISO 8601 period end date, exclusive (YYYY-MM-DD).

ParsedFittedCoefficient.description: One coefficient in a fitted model.

coefficients.description: Per-feature coefficients as a LIST of `{feature, weight}` objects. NOTE: this is a LIST OF OBJECTS, not a flat map. Correct shape: ```yaml coefficients: - { feature: "Weather_Temp", weight: 0.012 } - { feature: "Wind_MPH", weight: 0.035 } ```

intercept.description: Model intercept (bias term added before features × coefficients).

method.description: Method identifier. NOTE: this field is called `method`, NOT `type` (a common mistake). Legal values: `"linear"` | `"logistic"`.

output_bound.description: Phase 3H.1: optional output clamp applied after the link function. Per ADR-0017 Decision 2: either or both of `min`/`max` may be set; validator MC2070 rejects `min >= max` when both are present.

transforms.description: Phase 3H.2 (ADR-0018): optional adstock + saturation transform declarations applied to feature values before standardization + coefficient multiplication. Per Decision 7's binding pipeline order: feature → adstock → saturation → standardization → coefficient. Validators MC2071-MC2076 (and MC2077 if reached) cover correctness.

ParsedFixture.description: One named per-test fixture under `test_fixtures:`. Fixtures inherit the same source-XOR-inline shape as `canonical_inputs:`, but carry a `name:` so golden tests can reference them.

ParsedGoldenTest.description: Inline golden test entry. `coord` is a flat map of dim-name → element-name.

expect.description: Either `expect: 11500.0` (exact) or `expect_within_epsilon: { value: ..., epsilon: ... }` (tolerant). Exactly one of `expect` / `expect_within_epsilon` must be set; validator enforces.

fixture.description: Phase 3C: optional reference to a `test_fixtures` entry by name. When set, the named fixture's rows are applied (override semantic) on top of `canonical_inputs` before this golden runs. When unset, only `canonical_inputs` apply.

default.description: Optional flag: this hierarchy is the dimension's default. If no hierarchy under a given dimension is marked default, the first declared hierarchy becomes default (mirrors `DimensionBuilder`).

dimension.description: Dimension name (must reference a declared dimension).

ParsedInlineRows.description: Inline `rows:` payload for a `ParsedInputSet`. Each inner cell is a [`ParsedRowCell`] (string OR number OR bool) so dim columns (string) and the value column (numeric / bool) coexist on the same row.

ParsedInputSet.description:
One declared input set (`canonical_inputs:` or one entry of `test_fixtures:`). The block declares the column layout once and then either points at a sibling CSV file (`source:`) OR carries the rows inline (`inline:`). Exactly one of `source` / `inline` must be set — the resolve-inputs stage enforces this and emits a structural error if both / neither are set.

`columns:` is required for both forms. The last column name is reserved as the cell value (literal `"value"` per ADR-0006 amendment #19's alternate-route flag); every other column must match a dimension name declared in the model.

inline.description: Inline rows. Each inner Vec must have `columns.len()` entries. Per ADR-0006 Decision 1: tabular inline form only (per-row inline dropped pre-acceptance).

source.description: Sibling CSV file path, resolved relative to the YAML model file's directory. Path-escapes (`../`) are rejected per ADR-0006 amendment #18 (MC2022 with a path-escape variant).

ParsedIsElementBody.description:
`is_element(Dim, "Element")` — narrow numeric indicator.

`dimension` is a bare identifier (the dimension name); `element` is a quoted string literal. Per Phase 3I item 1 W4, string literals are allowed only as this second arg — a parse-time guarantee enforced by the formula parser.

ParsedLagBody.description: `lag(measure, periods)` — the periods argument is an expression.

ParsedLookupRefBody.description: `lookup("table", key1, key2, ...)` — Phase 3I item 3 made the key expression list variadic. A 1-element vec corresponds to the original Phase 3G single-key shape.

ParsedLookupTable.description:
Lookup table keyed by dimension element (Phase 3G).

Phase 3I item 3 made `key_dimension` optional and added the multi-key `key_dimensions: Vec<String>` field. Exactly one of the two must be set: - `key_dimension: Market` — single-key (Phase 3G shape, unchanged). - `key_dimensions: ["Market", "Time"]` — multi-key (Phase 3I).

MC2050 fires if both are set; MC2051 fires if any element name contains the pipe separator; MC2052 fires if a key has the wrong arity for the declared key_dimensions.

key_dimension.description: Single-key form. Backward compat with Phase 3G.

ParsedMeasure.description: A measure declaration. The measure dimension's element list is **derived from this section** during validation — measures appear once, not duplicated in `dimensions[Measure].elements`.

aggregation.description: `"Sum" | "WeightedAverage" | "Min" | "Max"`. Phase 3J Indicator measures default to Sum if omitted (per Decision 7 W7); explicit override permitted.

data_type.description: `"F64" | "I64" | "Bool" | "Category"`. Phase 3A's Acme uses F64 only; the others are forward surface. Phase 3J Indicator measures default to F64 (and may omit this field).

description.description: Optional human-readable description. Phase 3B's MC3002 lint fires when this is missing. Additive over ADR-0004's schema.

dimension.description: Phase 3J item 4: required when `role: "Indicator"`. Names the dimension whose element is checked at each coord. MC2064 fires at validate if missing on an Indicator measure.

element.description: Phase 3J item 4: required when `role: "Indicator"`. Names the element in `dimension:` that the indicator matches. MC2064 fires at validate if missing on an Indicator measure.

role.description: `"Input" | "Derived" | "Indicator"`. Phase 3J item 4 added `Indicator` (declarative reusable indicator measures, equivalent to the `is_element(Dim, "Element")` formula function per ADR-0016 Decision 7 + Amendment §6).

weight_measure.description: Required when `aggregation: "WeightedAverage"`. References another measure name from this same `measures:` block.

ParsedMeasureRefBody.description: Shared body for time-series functions that take a bare measure name.

ParsedNbinomBody.description: `nbinom_sf(k, mu, alpha)` / `nbinom_cdf(k, mu, alpha)` — Negative Binomial survival function and CDF in mean-dispersion parametrization. Shared body shape per docs/decisions/0031-nbinom-sf-formula-function.md §3 (Decision 1).

ParsedNormInvBody.description: `norm_inv(p, mu, sigma)` — inverse of normal-distribution CDF

ParsedOutputBound.description: Phase 3H.1: output clamp configuration on a fitted model. Both fields optional; one-sided clamps are valid (e.g., `min: 0` for non-negativity only). Per ADR-0017 Decision 4: if both are set, MC2070 fires when `min >= max`. NaN/infinite values are rejected by `serde_yaml` at parse time.

ParsedParamRefBody.description: Phase 3J item 3: `param(name)` — formula reference to a named scalar constant from the `parameters:` block. Structured form: `{ param: "q1_anchor_revenue" }`.

ParsedParameter.description: Phase 3J item 3: a named scalar constant referenced by `param(name)` in formulas. ADR-0016 Decision 6 binds v1 to `f64` values only; non- numeric values are rejected by the validator with MC2060.

ParsedPeriodIndexBody.description: Marker body for `period_index()` — a zero-field struct so the serde untagged dispatch doesn't match YAML null as a unit variant.

ParsedRollingAvgBody.description: `rolling_avg(measure, window)` — the window argument is an expression.

ParsedRowCell.description: One cell in an inline `canonical_inputs.rows[i]` / `test_fixtures[i].inline.rows[j]`. Wider than `ParsedScalar` (which is for rule constants and excludes strings on purpose) — inline rows mix string dim values with numeric cell values.

ParsedRule.description: A deterministic rule declaration. Phase 3D introduced the [`ParsedRuleBodyForm`] wrapper so `body:` may be authored either as a structured expression tree (`{ mul: [...] }`) or as a friendly formula string (`"Customers * AOV"`). Both forms produce identical [`ValidatedModel`]s — the validate stage parses the formula form into the structured tree before any downstream processing sees it.

allow_past_extrapolation.description: Phase 3J item 7 + Amendment §11: opt-in flag allowing `extrapolate_last_value(measure)` to be used at scopes other than `FutureLeaves`. Without this flag, MC2067 fires. Specific name (vs the proposed `extrapolate_anywhere`) is self-documenting.

body.description: Formula expression authored as a string (`"Customers * AOV"`) or as a structured s-expression tree. Both forms produce identical ValidatedModels. Example string bodies: - `"Customers * AOV"` - `"if(x > 0, x, 0)"` - `'predict("model_name", feature1, feature2)'` — NOTE the model name MUST be a quoted string literal, not a bare identifier (MC1009 fires otherwise).

declared_dependencies.description: Measures this rule reads. REQUIRED for dependency-graph correctness — omitting a measure that `body` actually reads causes the rule cycle / undeclared-dependency checks to fail. Include every bare measure name that appears in the body.

description.description: Optional human-readable description. Phase 3B's MC3003 lint fires when this is missing. Additive over ADR-0004's schema.

name.description: Unique name for this rule (distinct across the model). SEPARATE from `target_measure`: the name identifies the rule itself; the target_measure identifies which measure receives the computed value. A rule named "compute_revenue" might target the "Revenue" measure — the two names are independent on purpose.

scope.description: `"AllLeaves"` (Phase 3A's only supported scope). Phase 3F adds `"FutureLeaves"` for extrapolation rules; per ADR-0016 §11 the rule may opt into `allow_past_extrapolation` for relaxed scope.

target_measure.description: The measure this rule's body computes. NOTE: this field is SEPARATE from `name`. The same measure can only be targeted by one rule (a Derived measure has exactly one rule; MC2006 fires if missing, MC2007 if multiple).

ParsedRuleBody.description:
Structured expression-tree node. Each variant carries a distinguishing field name so the YAML stays JSON-shaped (per ADR-0004 Decision 1's safe subset bans on YAML tags). Serde's `untagged` enum dispatch picks the variant by which field name is present.

Phase 3E adds comparison, logical, and function variants (17 new). Phase 3F adds time-series variants (5 new). Phase 3G adds reference-data variants (4 new).

anyOf.description: `period_index()` — 0-based position in Time dim. Wrapped in a struct to prevent serde's untagged dispatch from matching YAML null as this unit variant.
`exp(x)` — Euler's number raised to the power of x
`nbinom_sf(k, mu, alpha)` — Negative Binomial survival function `P(X > floor(k))` in mean-dispersion parametrization.
`nbinom_cdf(k, mu, alpha)` — Negative Binomial CDF `P(X ≤ floor(k))`.
`norm_inv(p, mu, sigma)` — inverse of standard normal CDF.
`is_element(Dim, "Element")` — returns 1.0 if current coord's element in `Dim` is `"Element"`, 0.0 otherwise.

`{ str_literal: "Houston" }` (structured form) or `"Houston"` (formula form). Per ADR-0016 Decision 2, `Str` values exist only in expression evaluation; the validator rejects Str-typed bodies (MC2058), Str-in-arithmetic (MC1026), Str-in-numeric-comparison (MC1028), and Str-in-truthy-context (MC1027).

`{ current_element: "Channel" }` (structured form) or `current_element(Channel)` (formula form). Returns the current coordinate's element name in `dim` as `ScalarValue::Str`. At consolidated coords, returns Null.

`{ param: "q1_anchor_revenue" }` (structured form) or `param(q1_anchor_revenue)` (formula form). Resolves to the `f64` value declared on the model's `parameters:` block. Validate ensures the name is declared (MC2062 if missing).
Read `measure` from the named scenario at the current coord. Validator (MC2065) catches unknown scenario names.

LOCF (last-observation-carry-forward). Scans backward through the Time dim returning the most recent non-Null value of `measure`. Per ADR-0016 Decision 9 + Amendment §11, the validator (MC2067) requires the host rule to have `scope: FutureLeaves` OR `allow_past_extrapolation: true`. Amendment §5 reserves the future shape `extrapolate_last_value(measure, max_periods)`; v1 ships only the 1-arg form.
`avg_over(measure, dim)` — mean across leaf elements of `dim`.
`min_over(measure, dim)` — minimum across leaf elements of `dim`.
`max_over(measure, dim)` — maximum across leaf elements of `dim`.
`std_over(measure, dim)` — sample standard deviation (ddof=1) of `measure` across leaves of `dim`. Per ADR-0033 Amendment 3.

`count_over(measure, dim)` — count of non-null evaluated `measure` values across leaves of `dim`. Per ADR-0033 Amendment 2, the measure is evaluated at every leaf (same dispatch as `sum_over`), not counted from already-materialized store entries.

`wilson_ci_lower(p, n)` — Wilson 95% confidence-interval lower bound on a binomial proportion `p = k/n`. Both arguments are arbitrary numeric expressions (mirrors `norm_cdf` shape). Invalid inputs (n ≤ 0, p ∉ [0,1], NaN any arg) return Null per ADR-0033 Decision 3 and ADR-0031 Amendment 2.

ParsedRuleBodyForm.description:
Phase 3D: the YAML-faithful authoring form for a rule body. `Formula` holds the source text (`"Customers * AOV"`); `Structured` holds the existing s-expression-shaped tree.

`serde(untagged)` dispatches by YAML node kind: a scalar string maps to `Formula(_)`; a mapping maps to `Structured(_)`. Order matters — string must come first so a YAML scalar never accidentally tries the `Structured` mapping branch first. (Acceptance amendment per the Phase 3D handoff §"Phase 3D scope" item 2.)

The wrapper lives in [`ParsedModel`] only; [`ValidatedModel`] flattens to bare [`ParsedRuleBody`] so downstream stages (`compile`, `lint`, `inspect`) have no awareness of formula authoring form.

anyOf.description: `body: "Customers * AOV"`. Parsed by the formula parser at validate time; emits MC1003–MC1006 on syntax failure.
`body: { mul: [{ ref: "Customers" }, { ref: "AOV" }] }`. The structured form predates Phase 3D and continues to load unchanged.

ParsedSaturationSpec.description: Phase 3H.2: per-feature saturation spec. Tagged-enum with `#[serde(tag = "type")]` so unknown saturation types fire a serde deserialization error at parse time (Step 1 W2; supersedes MC2077 when serde catches first).

ParsedScalar.description: `Const` payload. `f64` and `i64` are the common shapes; `bool` is included for forward-compat. Phase 3E adds `Null` so formulas can reference it explicitly (e.g., `if(cond, value, Null)`).

ParsedScenarioRefBody.description: Phase 3J item 6: `scenario_ref(measure, "ScenarioName")` — read `measure` from the named scenario at the current coord.

ParsedStatusThreshold.description: Status threshold configuration with ordered bands (Phase 3G).

ParsedStrLiteralBody.description: Phase 3J item 1: string literal in expression evaluation. Authored as `{ str_literal: "Houston" }` in the structured YAML form, or as `"Houston"` in the friendly-formula form (the formula parser produces a `StrLiteral` node when a quoted string appears in primary position).

ParsedThresholdBand.description: One band within a status threshold. The last band must have `max: None` (unbounded above).

ParsedTransforms.description: Phase 3H.2 (ADR-0018): per-feature adstock + saturation transform block declared on a fitted model. Both inner lists default to empty so an empty `transforms: {}` declaration is permissive (Step 1 W4).

ParsedUnaryBody.description: Shared body for unary operators (not, abs).

ParsedWAvgOverBody.description: `wavg_over(value_measure, dim, weight_measure)` — weighted average across leaf elements of `dim`. Phase 3I item 5.

ParsedWilsonBody.description: `wilson_ci_lower(p, n)` / `wilson_ci_upper(p, n)` — Wilson 95% confidence-interval bounds on a binomial proportion. Shared body shape per ADR-0033 Decision 2; both arguments are arbitrary numeric expressions (mirrors `norm_cdf`'s shape, not `_over`'s bare-identifier constraint — Wilson's args are scalars, not measure references).
